bead.title: routing P1: ddx applies failure-classification table; single one-hop middle->max escalation + per-pool cap
bead.description: ddx maps each outcome per the FEAT-004 addendum table; owns the single middle->max escalation (only on typed genuine failure); per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap prevents multi-worker stampede; quota-exhausted -> global pool cooldown not per-bead. Depends on escalation-owner decision + shared classifier. See fizeau docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md Addendum for the normative failure-classification table + tier policy.
bead.acceptance: 1. ddx classifies each outcome per the table (table test). 2. exactly one middle->max, only on genuine failure; no_changes/dirty/transport/quota/auth do NOT escalate. 3. per-pool max-attempt cap test (concurrent failures). 4. quota-exhausted -> global cooldown test. 5. cd ddx/cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green. 6. lefthook passes.

events.body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"feat-004 addendum failure-classification table inlined in bead body: Four blockers prevent autonomous execution: (1) the normative failure-classification table lives only in fizeau/helix FEAT-004 addendum — cross-repo reference not self-contained per P7; (2) 'escalation-owner decision' is unresolved with no inlined text or bead ID; (3) 'shared classifier' has no bead ID or Go interface contract; (4) 'per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap' and 'global pool cooldown' introduce concepts absent from the codebase with undefined parameters (pool scope, cap value, cooldown duration, trigger conditions).","fingerprint":"a0ab3a7e4355f54a550fbc592b68b1d9fb772153ab53ab088461411f29df2e00","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"a0ab3a7e4355f54a550fbc592b68b1d9fb772153ab53ab088461411f29df2e00"}
